title: A system and method for dispensing a substance
abstract: The invention relates to a system and method for dispensing a substance, such as a drinkable fluid. The system comprises a camera which is arranged to obtain images and a processor arranged to analyse images. On the basis of the image analysis, the processor is arranged to control dispensing arrangement to dispense an amount of a substance, such as a beverage, into a receptacle. In an embodiment, the image processing is arranged to detect images relating to a surface or meniscus of a substance being dispensed and determine whether this indicates that a "full level" in the receptacle has been reached. When the full level is reached, dispensing is halted.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS : 1. A system for dispensing a substance into a receptacle, the system comprising a processing arrangement that is configured to: carry out an analysis of an initial electronic image; and control a dispensing arrangement based on the analysis of the initial electronic image such that the dispensing arrangement dispenses an amount of the substance into a receptacle. 2. A system in accordance with claim 1, the processing arrangement being configured to carry out analysis of at least one subsequent image and to control the dispensing arrangement based on the analysis of the at least one subsequent image. 3. A system in accordance with claim 2, wherein the processing arrangement is configured to analyse the images to detect images relating to a surface or meniscus of a substance being dispensed and to determine whether a full level of the receptacle has been reached. 4. A system in accordance with any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the processing arrangement is arranged to analyse the electronic image to detect the presence of a receptacle and, if a receptacle is detected, the dispensing arrangement is arranged to commence dispensing of the substance. 5. The system as claimed in claim 1, wherein the processing arrangement is such that the analysis of the initial electronic image comprises examining an image storage arrangement for a reference image of the receptacle, wherein the reference image depicts the receptacle with the predetermined amount of the substance. 6. The system as claimed in claim 5, wherein the 

processing arrangement is arranged to process at least one subsequent image of the receptacle to control the dispensing of the substance into the receptacle. 7. The system as claimed in claim 5 or claim 6, wherein the processing arrangement is arranged to process at least one subsequent image of the receptacle and the reference image of the receptacle to control the dispensing arrangement. 8. The system as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the processing arrangement is further configured to process a signal from a load cell and control the dispensing arrangement such that it dispenses the predetermined amount of the substance into the receptacle . 9. The system as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the processing arrangement is configured to communicate, via a data communication network, with a computing device that is remote to the system, wherein the processing arrangement and the computing device communicate such that the computing device can initiate dispensing of the predetermined amount of the substance into the receptacle. 10. A method for dispensing a substance into a receptacle, the method comprising the steps of: a processing arrangement carrying out an analysis of an initial electronic image; and the processing arrangement controlling a dispensing arrangement based on the analysis of the initial electronic image such that the dispensing arrangement dispenses an amount of the substance into a receptacle. 11. A method in accordance with claim 10, comprising the steps of carrying out an analysis of at least one 

further electronic image. 12. A method in accordance with claim 11, wherein the step of processing of the images is arranged to detect a surface or meniscus of the substance being dispensed and determine its positon, the dispensing arrangement is arranged to dispense until a predetermined full level position is reached. 13. A method in accordance with claim 12, wherein a method comprises the step of determining the full level relative to a part of the receptacle. 14. A method in accordance with any one of claims 10 to 13, wherein the processing of the image comprises the step of examining the image for the presence of a receptacle, and, if a receptacle is determined to be present, commencing dispensing of the substance. 15. The method as claimed in claim 10, wherein the step of the processing arrangement carrying out the analysis of the initial electronic image comprises the step of examining an image storage arrangement for a reference image of the receptacle, wherein the reference image depicts the receptacle with the predetermined amount of the substance. 16. The method as claimed in claim 15, further comprising the step of the processing arrangement processing at least one subsequent image of the receptacle to control the dispensing of the substance into the receptacle . 17. The method as claimed in claim 16, further comprising the step of the processing arrangement processing at least one subsequent image of the receptacle and the reference image of the receptacle to control the 

dispensing arrangement. 18. The method as claimed in any one of claims 10 to 17, further comprising the step of the processing arrangement processing a signal from a load cell and control the dispensing arrangement such that it dispenses the predetermined amount of the substance into the receptacle . 19. The method as claimed in any one of claims 10 to 18, further comprising the step of the processing arrangement communicating, via a data communication network, with a computing device that is remote to the system, wherein the processing arrangement and the computing device communicate such that the computing device can initiate dispensing of the predetermined amount of the substance into the receptacle.
